body.verdict: LOCK 016 correctly blocks runtime on Echo's machine. All runtime flags false. Metadata-only contract. Approved.

review_items.description: defineRuntimeBoundary() returns boundary metadata with all runtime flags false. No dynamic capability probing. No eval(), no new Function(), no dynamic imports. Aligns with Tripp.Control v0.1 security boundaries.
Metadata-only contract. No require(), import(), or fetch() calls. Boundary is a pure data structure.
Returns metadata object. No fs.writeFile, no database writes, no file system side effects. Stateless.
All runtime flags false - these should be frozen constants, not mutable booleans that could be flipped by drift or accident.
review_items.suggestion: Define as const RUNTIME_FLAGS = Object.freeze({ execute: false, networkAccess: false, fsWrite: false, modelCall: false }) and have defineRuntimeBoundary() return by reference.

review_items.description: defineRuntimeBoundary(input) accepts input but spec does not define what input contains or how it is validated.
review_items.suggestion: Define input schema explicitly. Validate and normalize. If unused, document: 'input reserved for future adapter selection; not consumed in v0.1'.

review_items.description: No mechanism to detect if future code changes flip a runtime flag to true. The adapter contract is an interface - implementations could override.
review_items.suggestion: Add drift rule to governance config: RUNTIME_ADAPTER_BOUNDARY.flags any flag true = halt pipeline, require operator review. Implement as pre-flight check.

review_items.description: LOCK 016 correctly follows the pattern from LOCK 001-015: define contract first, verify safety, implement later. Adapter boundary sits at correct layer between governance spine and future runtime.

body.local_safety_summary: LOCK 016 passes all 6 safety checks. Three hardening recommendations (constants freeze, input schema, drift detection) are non-blocking. No risk of runtime execution, network access, or persistence writes on Echo's local machine.
